2606.09707.title: BrainSurgery: Reproducible and Reliable Declarative Weight Manipulations for Model Editing and Upcycling
2606.09707.paper_summary: As deep learning models scale, managing, inspecting, and modifying large checkpoints has become increasingly challenging. Researchers often need to alter model weights for layer restructuring, precision casting, low-rank factorization, and architectural debugging, yet these workflows often rely on fragile ad-hoc Python scripts. Here, we introduce BrainSurgery, a tool for robust and reproducible "tensor surgery" on neural network checkpoints, and provide a system demonstration covering four examples and three case studies from model upcycling to LoRA extraction. By abstracting storage formats and memory management, BrainSurgery executes complex transformations through declarative YAML plans. It supports structural modifications, mathematical transformations, and tensor reshaping through expressive regex and structural targeting, while built-in assertions validate tensor shapes, data types, and values to prevent silent errors. We envision that BrainSurgery will provide a strong foundation for future research through its reproducible and validated operations.
2606.09707.paper_summary_zh: 隨著深度學習模型的擴展，管理、檢查和修改大型檢查點變得越來越具挑戰性。<lb/>研究人員經常需要改變模型權重以進行層重構、精度轉換、低秩分解和架構調試，但這些工作流程往往依賴脆弱的臨時 Python 腳本。<lb/>在此，我們介紹 BrainSurgery，一個用於神經網絡檢查點的穩健且可重現的「張量手術」工具，並提供一個系統演示，涵蓋四個範例和三個案例研究，從模型升級到 LoRA 提取。<lb/>通過抽象存儲格式和內存管理，BrainSurgery 通過聲明式 YAML 計劃執行複雜的轉換。<lb/>它支持結構修改、數學轉換和張量重塑，通過表達式正則表達式和結構定位來實現，同時內建的斷言驗證張量形狀、數據類型和值，以防止靜默錯誤。<lb/>我們預見 BrainSurgery 將通過其可重現和經過驗證的操作為未來的研究提供堅實的基礎。
2606.09707.author: Gianluca Barmina et.al.
2606.09707.authors: Gianluca Barmina, Annemette Broch Pirchert, Andrea Blasi Núñez, Lukas Galke Poech, Peter Schneider-Kamp
